findings.impact: Establishes prevalence floor for AUPRC benchmark (0.0935).

findings.observation: schema_v1.1 reference graph: 363K treats, 109K bills_with, 76K collaborates edges.
findings.impact: Confirms fold-safe heterogeneous graph artifacts for GNN training.
findings.follow_up_action: Proceed to tabular and graph baselines.

findings.observation: Logistic Regression outperformed CatBoost, RF, and RF+Centrality on mean AUPRC.
findings.impact: LR becomes primary tabular benchmark; Gate G3 passed.
findings.follow_up_action: Test whether graph structure adds value beyond tabular features.

findings.observation: GraphSAGE failed to beat Logistic Regression (5-fold AUPRC 0.6530 vs 0.6810).
findings.impact: Gate G4 failed; homophilic GraphSAGE insufficient for fraud heterophily.

findings.observation: Mean neighbor aggregation outperformed max aggregation on fold-0 HPO.
findings.evidence: Mean AUPRC 0.631 vs max 0.598 across 12 HPO configs.
findings.impact: Select mean aggregation for future homophilic GNN baselines.
findings.follow_up_action: Document in graph model design guidelines.

findings.evidence: Layer-2 mean AUPRC 0.622 vs layer-3 mean 0.607.
findings.impact: Prefer shallow GNNs on 4GB GPU constraint; reduces overfitting risk.
findings.follow_up_action: Cap default depth at 2 layers for HPO quick mode.

findings.observation: Hidden dimension 32 achieved best fold-0 AUPRC (0.6413).
findings.impact: Smaller models generalize better than h128 on this corpus.

findings.observation: R-GCN eval benchmark AUPRC 0.6542; does not beat LR.
findings.follow_up_action: Fusion model or richer edge features.

findings.observation: Prior rgcn_benchmark.json (AUPRC 0.6159) used max_epochs=2 and is invalid for baseline comparison.
findings.impact: Withdrawn from publication narrative until republication-grade benchmark completes.
findings.follow_up_action: Run rgcn_pub_v2 with max_epochs=100, patience=10, full HPO grid.

findings.observation: Publication-grade R-GCN (rgcn_pub_v2): h128_L2_d0.4 AUPRC 0.6542±0.0464; beats GraphSAGE (0.6530) but not LR (0.6810).
findings.impact: Gate G5 still failed vs LR; marginal lift over GraphSAGE not significant (p=1.0).
findings.follow_up_action: Phase 6 fusion model; do not cite invalid 2-epoch benchmark.

findings.observation: logistic_regression 5-fold AUPRC 0.6810±0.0389; does not beat LR baseline.
findings.impact: Updates best-model ranking and publication story.
findings.follow_up_action: Sync research docs; compare significance tests.

decisions.decision: Adopt schema_v1.1 as primary heterogeneous graph schema.
decisions.alternatives_considered: v1b (bene-physician edges), v2 (diagnosis nodes), v4 (physician filter).
decisions.reason: Matches literature PP threshold; validated reference counts; fold-safe construction.
decisions.expected_impact: Reproducible graph artifacts for all models.

decisions.decision: Provider-provider collaborates edges require >= 2 shared beneficiaries.
decisions.reason: Reduces noise from single co-occurrence; G2 reference count 75,604 edges.
decisions.expected_impact: Sparsifies PP graph; improves signal-to-noise for GNN message passing.

decisions.decision: Claims are not materialized as graph nodes.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Claim-level heterogeneous graph; provider-only projected graph.
decisions.reason: Provider-level prediction target; claim features aggregated to provider nodes.
decisions.expected_impact: Manageable graph size; aligns with CMS fraud detection unit.

decisions.decision: Proceed to R-GCN after GraphSAGE Gate G4 failure.
decisions.alternatives_considered: HGT, GAT, deeper GraphSAGE, tabular-only ensemble.
decisions.reason: GraphSAGE homophily assumption likely limits performance; relations have distinct semantics.
decisions.expected_impact: Test whether relation-aware modeling beats LR AUPRC 0.6810.

decisions.decision: Provider-disjoint stratified 5-fold CV (seed=42) for all evaluation.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Random split, temporal split, beneficiary-disjoint CV.
decisions.reason: Prevents provider leakage; stratification preserves fraud prevalence per fold.
decisions.expected_impact: Conservative generalization estimate; comparable across models.

decisions.decision: Primary metric: AUPRC; secondary: ROC-AUC, F1, Precision, Recall, Recall@K.
decisions.alternatives_considered: ROC-AUC primary, F1 primary, provider-level AP only.
decisions.reason: Class imbalance (9.35% fraud); AUPRC sensitive to ranking quality.
decisions.expected_impact: Consistent model selection across phases and gates.

decisions.decision: Invalidate and archive rgcn_benchmark.json produced with max_epochs=2 (rgcn_v1 smoke-scale).
decisions.alternatives_considered: Keep 0.6159 AUPRC for G5 reporting; partial HPO continuation.
decisions.reason: Audit found 2-epoch cap is not comparable to GraphSAGE (max_epochs=100) or tabular baselines.
decisions.expected_impact: Re-run publication-grade R-GCN HPO and 5-fold benchmark before any G5 or paper claims.

project_status.pending_experiments: Fusion model (tabular + graph); GNNExplainer case studies; optional R-GCN full HPO if eval-mode results insufficient
project_status.known_risks: Tabular LR dominates GNNs; class imbalance drives high recall/low precision; 4GB GPU limits model depth; val providers require inductive inference graphs
project_status.next_action: Implement fusion or richer edge features; document why GNNs underperform LR

publication_story.research_problem: Detect fraudulent Medicare providers using heterogeneous relational data (providers, beneficiaries, physicians) with explainable graph-based models.
publication_story.literature_gap: Prior CMS fraud work relies on tabular features or homogeneous graphs; relation-specific heterogeneous modeling under provider-disjoint CV is under-tested.
publication_story.hypotheses: H1: Graph structure improves AUPRC beyond tabular LR. H2: Relation-aware R-GCN beats homophilic GraphSAGE. H3: PP collaborates edges carry fraud signal beyond treats/bills_with.
publication_story.experimental_evidence: G3: LR AUPRC 0.6810 beats CatBoost/RF. G4 failed: GraphSAGE AUPRC 0.6530. G5 failed: R-GCN AUPRC 0.6542. Mean aggregation and 2-layer depth preferred for GraphSAGE.
publication_story.current_conclusions: Strong tabular baseline (LR) sets high bar. GraphSAGE homophilic message passing does not overcome feature dominance. R-GCN under initial benchmark also below LR; fusion or explainability may be required for publication-grade lift.
